milestone: Draft Asset Architecture, Phase D: migration activation
slice: Wires the already-built AssetStore.migrateFieldsOnSave (Phase A) into World Builder v2's own existing debounced save path, so a World Project already saved before Phase B/C shipped -- still holding raw embedded data: URIs -- starts shrinking to durable vihu-asset: references one save at a time, with no proactive sweep and no version flag: format-detection alone (is this field data:-prefixed or not?) decides whether a field needs migrating, exactly like every other read path in this feature already works. A new _collectMigrationAccessors(project) walks every known image-bearing field (project.files[path] top-level entries not ending in .json/.md -- Identity Thumbnail/Hero, Assets-screen slots; every Scene Layer's own .image field; every Experience's properties.imageSrc/graphicSrc) and returns {get,set} accessor pairs; a new _scheduleAssetMigration(), debounced 1500ms and called from _persist()'s own success branch (mirroring _scheduleCloudSync's established shape exactly), calls AssetStore.migrateFieldsOnSave('builder', project.id, accessors) in the background and, only if anything actually changed, performs one more plain ProjectStore.save() plus a cloud-sync nudge so the smaller payload reaches localStorage and, once signed in, the cloud backup too. A real subtlety, handled deliberately rather than accepted as a duplicate-upload cost: a decoration Scene Layer mirrored from an Experience (sourceExperienceId/contentSlot set, Builder V3.1's Universal Experience Authoring) is skipped as its own accessor entirely -- migrating it independently would call put() a second time for byte-identical content (the exact 'one upload becomes two' the original architecture plan's own note warned against) -- instead the source Experience's own accessor propagates its freshly-migrated reference directly onto the already-known mirrored Layer object with no second put() call, verified end-to-end (the mirrored Layer's .image ends up the SAME reference string as the source Experience's imageSrc, never a distinct one). A genuine, if narrow, race was found and closed at its real root, in the shared js/assetStore.js module itself (so both surfaces benefit, not just this one): migrateFieldsOnSave's own put()-then-set() step now does a compare-and-swap -- it only rewrites a field if it STILL holds the exact bytes just migrated at the moment the put() resolves, so a field genuinely replaced by a fresh, unrelated upload (Replace Artwork, a new Experience image) while the migration's own IndexedDB write was still in flight is left completely untouched rather than silently reverted to a stale reference; verified via a direct, fully-timing-controlled unit-level proof against the real (unmodified) function. Verified via a new 24-assertion Playwright suite spanning both surfaces: a Project hand-seeded with legacy data: URIs (a top-level Identity asset, a standalone Scene Layer image, and an Experience+its mirrored Layer sharing one legacy image) reopened after a real page reload, then a real, unrelated edit (World Name) triggers _persist() -- after the debounce settles, every field is confirmed migrated to a real vihu-asset:builder: reference that resolves back to a real local blob: URL, the mirrored Layer and its source Experience share the identical reference (no double upload), and a second later save is confirmed a genuine no-op (idempotent); the compare-and-swap race-fix proof; and the same discipline exercised on Studio's own surface (Creator), confirming slide._imageDataURL/placeContent[id].dataURL/elementOverrides[id].image all migrate correctly through the analogous fix in js/projectManager.js, while slide.thumbnail is confirmed to deliberately NOT migrate -- a disclosed Phase D scope decision, since several of its own read sites (page-strip/My-Projects/Magic-Card-Home thumbnails) were never rewired to resolve a reference and thumbnails are small, derived, regenerable data nowhere near the scale that caused the original quota bug. One real, pre-existing Phase C read-side gap was also found and fixed in the same pass, unrelated to migration itself: js/pageDesigner.js's Cover/Hook/End Image Manager panel's own preview assigned s._imageDataURL directly with no resolve step -- fixed by reusing s.image.src (the already-loaded, already-resolved Image object the same branch's own condition already confirms exists), rather than adding a second async resolve path. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) and the existing Phase A/B/C Playwright suites all pass unchanged. Cache-bust bumped to ?v=0103 on every tag since this changes worldBuilderApp.js (loaded from this same index.html). Phase E (full end-to-end verification, docs, cleanup) remains its own separately-scoped, not-yet-started increment.
